title: helianthemum chamaecistus subsp. hirsutum
intro: Helianthemum chamaecistus subsp. hirsutum, also known as the hairy rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a perennial herb with woody stems and yellow or pink flowers, commonly found in rocky areas. The hairy rockrose has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can help to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin conditions, offering potential health benefits for skin and respiratory issues.
